The chapter now ends after Theo's departure on Draco making tea and returning to Hermione in the library, not on a solitary window or darkness contemplation.

Three composed-register passages were cited. (1) The aphoristic 'We're standing in the proof' line (snapshot:87) was already absent from the current chapter after P1 compression — no action needed. (2) The licensing exchange (current:177-179) had Theo's 'I didn't say acceptable. I said real.' replaced with 'No, it doesn't. But being right about it hasn't helped me get a lease.' — grounding the exchange in concrete personal frustration rather than polished binary distinction. (3) Theo's exit sequence (current:215-229) was substantially rewritten: the pre-announced mic-drop delivery replaced with a back-and-forth ('You look different' / 'Different how?'), aborted phrasing ('Like you —' He stopped.), and the neat thesis 'You look like someone who lives here' / 'Today you don't' replaced with a two-part observation delivered across physical action with no concluding seal.

Standalone 'Sure.' at line 59 (Hermione) replaced with 'Mm-hm.' — a non-verbal-adjacent acknowledgment that is not any character's example_lines entry. Full sweep confirmed no remaining verbatim matches against any example_lines entry in the style bible (checked all six character profiles).

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 10 now moves through a clear three-step progression: the post-drawing-room routine settles, Draco crosses a new line by caring for Hermione in the library, and Theo witnesses the change and names it. The repeated warmth recaps, patterned phrasing, and darkness-ending template were removed while preserving the chapter's domestic tenderness and continuity into chapter 11.

Both findings resolved. The composed-register finding required roughening two of three cited passages (the third was already absent from the current chapter). The licensing exchange now grounds Theo's rebuttal in personal stakes rather than a neat binary, and Theo's exit now arrives through conversational friction — aborted phrasing, a question from Draco, physical action between beats — instead of a pre-announced thesis delivery. The example-line finding required replacing two instances of verbatim 'Sure.' (a Theo calibration-only line) with character-appropriate alternatives. No new example-line matches were introduced. Chapter heading contract, must-land beats, state deltas, and continuity are preserved.

Revised chapter 10 for prose/copyedit finish by stripping explanatory after-statements from the domestic aftermath beats and eliminating the repeated specific/particular emphasis scaffold while preserving the chapter's tender, quiet-state movement.
